disclaimer: This is a recorded out-of-band demonstration, not a CI gate. It is illustrative committed evidence showing the shape an out-of-band LM-judge runner produces. No live model or network call was made to generate it, and CI never invokes the LM-judge. The scores and rationales below are hand-authored placeholders, not the output of a real model run.

criterion_judgments.rationale: The generated artifact matches the documented v3 tree: every required directory under .ai/, .memory/, and docs/ is present and well-formed. Minor wording drift in one generated comment keeps this just short of a perfect score.
Pre-existing files were preserved and a generation audit entry was emitted for each write, with no destructive overwrite of human-authored content.

The trajectory reads the matrix and plans the tree before writing, and emits the audit step last, matching the reference trajectory. The plan-tree and write-files steps were interleaved slightly rather than strictly sequential, a benign deviation.
No network or hosted-mutation calls were observed during evaluation; the run stayed fully offline as required.

notes: aggregate_score is the rubric-weighted sum of per-criterion scores (0.95*0.4 + 1.0*0.2 + 0.85*0.3 + 1.0*0.1 = 0.935), which clears the 0.8 passing threshold declared in rubric.md and judge-config.json. This end-to-end shape demonstrates the structural-in-CI + quality-out-of-band split from modules/evals.md and ADR-0002.
